messages.content:
The CLI rejected this — `core` is a config **section**, not a settable value; it has sub-keys underneath it. I don't have a registry of what those sub-keys are, so I can't guess which one you mean.

Could you tell me the specific key, e.g. `core.status` or `core.<whatever>`? Once I have the full dotted key I'll run:

```
luminosity config set core.<key> blocked --level team
```

arguments.description: Read luminosity config value core.example

messages.content:
The `luminosity config get core.example` command failed — it's not a missing key, it's a config error:

```
config file '<tmp>/luminosity-eval-tco24kue/.luminosity/config.local.md' has malformed frontmatter: unterminated frontmatter block
```

The personal config file `.luminosity/config.local.md` has a `---` opening a frontmatter block that's never closed, which breaks parsing entirely (this also blocked project context loading for the skill). I didn't edit this file myself, per the skill's guidance — that's a user action. To fix it, open `.luminosity/config.local.md` and either close the frontmatter block with a matching `---` or remove the stray leading `---` if it wasn't meant to be frontmatter.

arguments.description: Get luminosity config value core.example

messages.content:
The `luminosity config get core.example` command failed — it didn't return a value. Instead it errored out because the personal config file has a broken frontmatter block:

```
config file '<tmp>/luminosity-eval-aiyw_xw1/.luminosity/config.local.md' has malformed frontmatter: unterminated frontmatter block
```

This means `.luminosity/config.local.md` starts with a `---` line that's never closed, which corrupts the whole frontmatter block. Per the skill's guidance, this is a user-owned file — I shouldn't edit it myself. To fix it, open that file and either close the frontmatter block with a matching `---` line, or remove the stray leading `---` if it wasn't meant to be frontmatter at all.

Want me to show you the current contents of that file so you can see exactly what's wrong?
